mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 0.5% signals monetary accommodation that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors rotate into equities seeking yield.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.41% as of decision date indicates ultra-low rate environment
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or forward returns in periods with FEDFUNDS < 0.5% do not exceed baseline by at least 2% annualized (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month payroll employment growth above 150,000 persons signals sustained labor market strength and consumer income expansion, driving positive equity returns over 60 trading days as earnings expectations rise and recession risk declines.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 145,128 thousand as of decision date indicates near-full employment
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite strong payroll growth, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening consumer demand and capital formation, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as forward earnings estimates are revised downward.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline > 2% month-over-month simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 465,513 million and HOUST at 1,323 thousand as of decision date
regime_hint: demand-side slowdown or early recession signal
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite joint demand contraction, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index (INDPRO) crossing above its 6-month moving average signals accelerating supply-side capacity utilization and productivity gains, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as cyclical equity valuations expand.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 103.8589 (2017=100) as of decision date
regime_hint: cyclical expansion with rising capacity utilization
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns following INDPRO crossover are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate by more than 0.3 percentage points within a 3-month window, combined with stable or declining federal funds rate, signals the Fed's shift toward accommodation in response to labor market weakness, producing positive equity returns over 20 trading days as rate-cut expectations increase.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months AND FEDFUNDS remains flat or declines
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.6% and FEDFUNDS at 0.41% as of decision date
regime_hint: monetary policy pivot toward easing amid labor market deterioration
failure_condition: The interaction coefficient between UNRATE change and FEDFUNDS level is not statistically significant (p > 0.10), or SPX produces negative returns in > 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerating below 2% annualized signals late-cycle demand exhaustion and forward earnings compression, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as investors reduce equity risk exposure in anticipation of recession.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 16,712.5 billion chained 2017 dollars as of decision date
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of instances with GDPC1 growth < 2%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate rising above 5.0% signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer purchasing power, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as earnings estimates are revised downward and recession risk premiums increase.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.6% as of decision date indicates labor market tightness
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or early-stage recession
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of instances with UNRATE > 5.0%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL) above 2.5% year-over-year, combined with federal funds rate remaining below 1.0%, signals monetary policy lag and real rate compression that inflates equity valuations through lower discount rates, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 242.348 and FEDFUNDS at 0.41% as of decision date
regime_hint: monetary accommodation with rising inflation expectations
failure_condition: The interaction coefficient between inflation and policy rate is not statistically significant (p > 0.10), or SPX produces negative returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite accommodative policy and inflation.
